content.problemStatement:
Given a string containing lowercase English letters and parentheses '(' and ')', find a valid parentheses string by removing the minimum number of parentheses. If no valid string can be formed except empty string, print -1. A string is valid if:


It contains only lowercase letters, or
It can be written as (A) where A is valid, or
It can be written as AB where A and B are valid

Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
Next TTT lines contain string SSS


Output Format

For each test case, print the valid string after removing minimum parentheses. If only empty string is possible, print -1.
constraints: SSS contains only ′(′,′)′'(', ')'′(′,′)′, and lowercase English letters
Sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ across all test cases ≤105\leq 10^5≤105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Removing first ')' makes string valid with (c) as proper pair 
Test Case 2: All brackets are invalid, only empty string possible, hence output -1 
Test Case 3: Remove first '(' to make all other brackets properly paired 
Test Case 4: Already valid string with proper bracket pairs
